files.name: Comments of NRG Energy ... signed Travis Kavulla.docx
files.path: sources/comments/files/20251119-5170__NRG-Energy-Inc/Comments of NRG Energy Interconnection of Large Loads signed Travis Kavulla .docx

summary: Supports the ANOPR's principles but proposes five discrete changes — most notably pricing interconnection capacity through gas-pipeline-style 'open seasons' rather than first-come queues.
key_points: Establish a rebuttable presumption that any load interconnection at 230 kV or above is a 'large load' subject to the standardized process.
Drop the proposed 20 MW size threshold.
Use open seasons to expedite interconnection and efficiently allocate scarce interconnection capacity (Kavulla's signature 'price the grid' argument).
Load and hybrid facilities responsible for 100% of the network-upgrade costs they cause.
Preserve the rights of large loads that already made commitments under state-prescribed procedures.

quotes.text: NRG proposes that the final rule ... provide for the use of open seasons to expedite large load interconnections and to efficiently allocate interconnection capacity to such interconnections.
